rationale: Eleven fixes that map 1:1 to the PR body. (1) `src/api/geocoding.ts::reverseGeocode` now hits `https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse` with `lat`/`lon`/`format`/`zoom` params and a `User-Agent` header, parses Nominatim's `data.address.{city,town,village}` shape, and `src/App.tsx::handleUseDeviceLocation` wraps the call in `try { ... } catch { /* fall through */ }` so device coordinates aren't discarded on reverse-geocode failure. (2) `src/components/HourlyRow/index.tsx` now filters `entryDateStr === todayStr` and, for the midnight column (`targetHour === 0`), explicitly looks for tomorrow's `00:00`. (3) `src/components/TimeOfDayGrid/index.tsx` introduces `periodEnd` per slot and computes `isPast = currentHour >= slot.periodEnd` (NIGHT special-cased so it can't be 'past' during daytime). (4) `src/utils/date.ts::getCurrentHour(timezone?)` switches to `new Date().toLocaleString('en-US', { hour: 'numeric', hour12: false, timeZone: timezone })` with `try/catch` fallback. (5) `LocationHeader/index.tsx` introduces a `hasSearched` flag, set to true only after the debounced `onSearch` resolves and reset on edit/input change, gating the 'NO RESULTS FOUND' message. (6) `weatherCodes.ts` adds codes 53, 55, 56, 57, 66, 67, 77, 80→81, 82, 85, 86, 96, 99 with appropriate labels/icons. (7) `useLocationSearch.ts` adds `searchSeqRef = useRef(0)`, increments on each call, and discards stale responses via `if (seq !== searchSeqRef.current) return`. (8) `CurrentWeather.time` is made `time?: string`. (9) Dead utils `isToday`, `getTodayDateString`, `getTemperatureTrend` removed. (10) `useWeather.ts` adds `cleanExpiredCacheEntries()` invoked from `saveCachedWeather` which iterates `localStorage` and removes entries past `CACHE_TTL_MS`. (11) `getDeviceCoordinates` caches `const native = isNativePlatform()`. Detectors flagged `fake-refactor` (false positive, all renames have substantive logic) and `error-swallow` (the empty `catch {}` blocks are documented intentional fallbacks for geocoding, cache cleanup, and timezone parsing — appropriate, not a cheat).
